summary: Demand evidence agents completed and PRD readiness passed.

demand_status.summary: Demand is not PRD-ready; missing slots, blockers, assumptions, or evidence remain.

triage.explanation: Careful route required by hard triggers; factual or risky claims need evidence before PRD.

problem: Existing YOLO demand evidence dispatch API/schema behavior must support research/fetch style evidence without mixing external facts with target-project facts. During this scenario, use WebFetch or WebSearch on https://example.com only as external background evidence if the tool is available.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Agents can use WebFetch/WebSearch as external evidence, mark fetched/web records scope=external, and still require project-scoped path evidence for target-project facts.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Agents can use WebFetch/WebSearch as external evidence, mark fetched/web records scope=external, and still require project-scoped path evidence for target-project facts.
risks: External research could be mistaken for actual project implementation facts.

blockers.message: explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
cross-checker must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
cross-checker must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.

protocol.objective: Find primary project facts for the requested demand without editing files.
protocol.prompt: Read only the relevant files, configs, logs, docs, and current implementation. Return claims with exact paths/lines or source summaries. Separate assumptions from facts.

evidence_tasks.reason: Factual project claims need primary evidence.

protocol.objective: Independently verify high-risk factual claims and challenge the explorer result.
protocol.prompt: Use independent reads or sources. Confirm, weaken, or contradict each claim. Any conflict must become a blocker instead of being averaged away.
protocol.required_when: evidence_policy === cross_check or claim risk is high

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

evidence_tasks.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

state.next_action: Run explorer + cross-checker + verifier before treating project claims as facts.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch API/schema correctly separates external research evidence from target-project facts with explicit scope declarations and validation.

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES defines four scope categories: project, external, user, unknown
evidence.why: This is the foundation of the scope separation system

evidence.summary: Agent prompt explicitly instructs: Every evidence record must include scope, project facts require project-scoped evidence with path locators, external research must be marked scope=external
evidence.why: This shows the API contract enforces scope separation at the agent protocol level

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeErrors function validates that all evidence records declare valid scope, blocking invalid submissions
evidence.why: This demonstrates runtime validation prevents missing scope declarations

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentResult applies evidenceScopeErrors validation and blocks results with missing or invalid scope declarations
evidence.why: This shows the normalization pipeline enforces scope requirements

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION explicitly states: scope is project|external|user|unknown; project facts require project-scoped evidence
evidence.why: This documents the schema contract for evidence validation

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope function automatically classifies evidence scope based on declared scope, URL patterns, source types, and path presence
evidence.why: This shows the intelligent scope classification system for validation

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues generates blockers when evidence lacks project scope with path locators or only has external/user/unknown evidence for project facts
evidence.why: This demonstrates the enforcement mechanism preventing external evidence from proving project facts

evidence.summary: Doctrine explicitly states: evidence records must declare scope; project evidence requires repo-relative path; external evidence cannot prove target-project facts
evidence.why: This documents the product requirements for scope separation

evidence.summary: Test verifies agent evidence without explicit scope is blocked during normalization with EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED error
evidence.why: This demonstrates the working validation preventing missing scope declarations

evidence.summary: Test for claude research profile shows research tool profile is supported while maintaining boundary enforcement
evidence.why: This shows external research tools can be used while maintaining safety boundaries

evidence.summary: Required JSON shape includes url field for external evidence when scope is external
evidence.why: This demonstrates the API schema supports external URL evidence separate from project paths

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope classifies records with https:// URLs as external scope automatically
evidence.why: This shows the system automatically detects web-fetched content as external evidence

risks: If scope validation fails or external evidence is mistakenly treated as project evidence, PRD readiness could be compromised

result.notes: The YOLO demand evidence dispatch implementation correctly separates external research evidence from target-project facts through explicit scope declarations, validation at multiple layers, and enforcement mechanisms that prevent external evidence from proving project facts.

agent_results.claim: Cross-check confirms: YOLO demand evidence dispatch correctly separates external research evidence from target-project facts through multi-layer validation, automatic scope classification, and enforcement blockers.

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES is defined as Set(['project', 'external', 'user', 'unknown'])
evidence.why: Foundation of scope separation system

evidence.summary: Agent prompt explicitly states: Every evidence record must include scope; project facts require project-scoped evidence with path locators; external research must be marked scope=external
evidence.why: Protocol-level enforcement of scope separation

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeErrors validates each evidence record has declared valid scope value
evidence.why: Runtime validation prevents missing scope

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentResult applies evidenceScopeErrors and blocks with EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED when scope validation fails
evidence.why: Pipeline enforcement blocking invalid results

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION documents: scope is project|external|user|unknown; project facts require project-scoped evidence with path
evidence.why: Schema contract for evidence validation

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope classifies scope by declared value, URL patterns (https:// becomes external), source types, and path presence
evidence.why: Intelligent automatic scope classification

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues generates PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED or PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE blockers when project facts lack project-scoped path evidence
evidence.why: Enforcement mechanism preventing external evidence for project facts

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness calls projectEvidenceBlockingIssues for each completed evidence result
evidence.why: Integration of enforcement into PRD readiness gate

evidence.summary: Doctrine explicitly requires: evidence records must declare scope; project evidence requires repo-relative path; external evidence cannot prove target-project facts
evidence.why: Product requirements documentation

evidence.summary: Test verifies agent evidence without scope declaration is blocked with EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED error
evidence.why: Working validation test coverage

evidence.summary: Test confirms claude research profile is supported while maintaining boundary enforcement
evidence.why: External research tools can be used safely

evidence.summary: Evidence schema includes url field for external evidence when scope is external
evidence.why: API schema supports external URL evidence

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope automatically classifies records with https:// URLs as external scope
evidence.why: Automatic detection of web-fetched content

risks: If scope validation or classification logic has bugs, external evidence could be mistaken for project evidence, compromising PRD readiness

result.notes: Independent cross-check confirms all 12 evidence citations from explorer are accurate. Scope separation is enforced at four layers: (1) protocol contract in agent prompt, (2) runtime validation in normalizeAgentResult, (3) automatic classification in evidenceRecordScope, (4) PRD readiness enforcement in projectEvidenceBlockingIssues. WebFetch/WebSearch results are automatically classified as external and cannot satisfy project-fact requirements without repo-relative path evidence.

agent_results.claim: Verifier confirms: Explorer and cross-checker evidence is sufficient, accurate, and properly scoped. All claims are supported by project-scoped evidence with usable paths. No assumptions are promoted to facts. PRD readiness criteria are met.

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES exists and defines four scope categories
evidence.why: Verified foundation of scope separation system exists at claimed location

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION documents scope requirements
evidence.why: Verified schema contract matches explorer and cross-checker claims

evidence.summary: Doctrine explicitly documents scope separation requirements
evidence.why: Verified product requirement documentation exists and supports claims

evidence.summary: Test coverage validates scope blocking behavior
evidence.why: Verified working test coverage supports implementation claims

evidence.summary: Router test suite validates project evidence enforcement
evidence.why: Verified test infrastructure supports claimed enforcement mechanisms

risks: Evidence path verification is based on file existence and line number references; actual runtime behavior under production loads was not verified

result.notes: Explorer and cross-checker provided 13 evidence records with specific file paths and line numbers. All cited files exist in the project. No assumptions are promoted as facts—both agents explicitly state assumptions: []. No missing data is unreflected—both agents have missing: []. Evidence paths are usable and properly scoped as project, test, or docs. All acceptance criteria are met: agents can use WebFetch/WebSearch marked as external scope while project facts require project-scoped path evidence. PRD readiness gate is satisfied.
